label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the minimum number of socks I have to pull out from a drawer in the dark to guarantee at least two matching pairs. The drawer has 12 red, 8 blue, and 6 green socks. Hmm, so there are three colors: red, blue, and green. First, let me recall the pigeonhole principle. It says that if you have more pigeons than pigeonholes, at least one pigeonhole has more than one pigeon. But here, I need two matching pairs. So maybe I need to consider the worst-case scenario where I pull out socks in a way that delays getting two matching pairs as much as possible. Let me think. For one pair, the worst case would be pulling out one sock of each color first. So with three colors, that's
